Q1 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33

A circular cross-section beam is subjected to transverse loading. The ratio of maximum shear stress developed in the beam to the average shear stress is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q2 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which one of the following is not a property of a material?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Proof resilience is the maximum energy absorbed by member within the elastic limit and depends on the volume of material. Hence, it is not a property of a material.

Q3 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The effective flexural stiffness of a cantilever beam of length L and flexural rigidity subjected to bending moment M as shown below, is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A circular rod of 300 mm length and 50 mm diameter is subjected to axial tensile load of 50 kN. The elongation in the length and the reduction in the diameter is recorded as 0.080 mm and 0.005 mm, respectively. Considering the applied axial load to be within the elastic limit, the ratio of bulk modulus to modulus of rigidity is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A stepped steel bar fixed at end P as shown in the figure is loaded at three points Q, R and S. If the cross-section area of segments PQ, QR and RS are 200 mm2, 400 mm2 and 100 mm2, respectively, then the segment having maximum axial compression and maximum axial tension are, respectively

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A hypothetical engineering stress-strain curve as shown in the figure, has three straight lines OA, AB and BC with coordinates O(0, 0), A(0.002, 180), B(0.0045, 240) and C(0.006, 240). If A denotes elastic limit and C denotes fracture point, then the modulus of resilience for the material is ______ kN/m2. [Round off to the nearest integer]

NAT
Correct answer: 180
Solution

180 (180 - 180)

Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A machine component made of a ductile material is subjected to tri-axial state of stress as shown in the figure. If the modulus of rigidity and Poisson’s ratio of the material is 25 GPa and 0.3, respectively. The volumetric strain in the component is ____ × 10–4. (Round off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 6.15
Solution

6.15 (6.10 - 6.20)

Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A rigid horizontal bar AD of length 3a is hinged at A and supported by two strings BN and CM as shown in the figure. Both the strings are made of same material and have equal cross-sectional area. If a force P is applied at end D, the ratio of tension developed in string BN to string CM is
______. [Round off to one decimal place]

NAT
Correct answer: 1.5
Solution

1.5 (1.5 - 1.5)

Solution

(a, b, d)

Correct answer: A
Solution

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

A composite bar made up of an elastic material having modulus of elasticity E and specific weight is hanging freely under its self weight as shown in the figure. If the segment QR is a uniform cylinder of cross-sectional area A and segment RS is a right circular cone of length L, the vertical deflection of point S is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

A sphere of an elastic material has volume 70000 mm3 is subjected to hydrostatic pressure of 160 MPa. Considering the modulus of elasticity and Poisson’s ratio of the material as 210 GPa and 0.3, respectively. The reduction in the volume of the sphere is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Q14 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A bimetallic stepped cylindrical bar of length 2 m is made by bonding steel (Young’s modulus = 200 GPa) and aluminium (Young’s modulus = 70 GPa) as shown in the figure, and is fixed at both ends (A and C).

The area of cross-section of segment AB and BC are 200 mm2 and 80 mm2 respectively. If a force F is applied at section B such that the section is displaced towards right by 0.1 mm, then the force F is _____ N. [Round off to the nearest integer]

NAT
Correct answer: 4560
Solution

4560 (4550 - 4570)

Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A cantilever beam AB of uniform cross-section 15 mm × 24 mm is loaded with a uniformly varying load and a force F = 900 N at a distance L from the fixed end A, as shown in the figure.

If the distance L is such that the bending moment at the fixed end is zero, then the magnitude of maximum bending stress in the beam is ______ MPa.

(Round off to the nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 94
Solution

94 (93 - 95)

Q16 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A simply supported beam of length 1.5 m and cross-section 200 mm × 200 mm is carrying a uniformly varying load as shown in the figure.

The maximum shear stress in the beam is ______ N/mm2 [Round off to three decimal places]

NAT
Correct answer: -5.625
Solution

-5.625 (-5.625 - -5.625)
